title: vachellia tanjorensis
intro: Vachellia tanjorensis, also known as the Indian thorn or Tanjore tree, is a species of tree in the Acacia genus native to India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Vachellia tanjorensis include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction or tea, which is consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
